differentials.name: unilateral congenital deafness (commonly missed) ◊  in 12% of children with congenital SNHL
differentials.raw: unilateral congenital deafness (commonly missed) ◊  in 12% of children with congenital SNHL

context: =  cochlear cavity malformation =  saccular defect / cavity in otic capsule in the position normally occupied by cochlea without recognizable modiolus, osseous spiral lamina, interscalar septum May be associated with:   recurrent bacterial meningitis, perilymphatic fistula of oval window √  cystic cochlea (= developed basal turn, middle + apical turn occupy common nondeveloped space) Type: (a)   incomplete partition type I =  cystic cochleovestibular malformation =  complete lack of partitioning of cochlea √  cystic structure at CT (b)  incomplete partition type II (see below) =  classic Mondi
